baseline.reason: Baseline incorrectly claims line 264 (inside 'if push fails' block) always frees task after line 263's queue push, misunderstanding the control flow. It frames the double-free as free-at-264 then free-at-271, but line 264 only executes on push failure. The actual root cause is that task is freed at lines 271/278 while still owned by task_queue/lltask_queue entries.

vastdb.reason: VASTDB correctly identifies the core double-free mechanism: task is freed by the caller while queues (task_queue, lltask_queue) still hold references. It describes two trigger paths (push failure and later error) accurately. Minor inaccuracy: it implies extract_lltask_from_task could return error after successful push, when the real error-after-push-survival path is the ff_safe_queue_pop_front failure at line 276.
